messages.content:
Answer the following Air Force question

SAMPLE OF BEHAVIOR:  Cite the purpose of Enlisted Performance Briefs (EPB) and Officer Performance Briefs (OPB).

 

Which of the following is TRUE concerning Enlisted Performance Brief?



Formal report that discusses responsibilities, standards, and expectations.

Large contributing factor for promotion consideration and school selection.

Required upon completion of formal training education under 20 weeks’ time.

Only necessary for members who will promote during the next calendar year.
The correct answer is: Large contributing factor for promotion consideration and school selection.

SAMPLE OF BEHAVIOR:  Discuss facets of an Airman’s or Guardian’s performance measured through DAF evaluations.

 

Which of the following BEST describes how Airmen and Guardians are evaluated?


Demonstrated performance in the 4 Major Performance Areas (MPAs): Executing the Mission, Leading People, Managing Resources, and Improving the Unit

Demonstrated performance in the 4 Main Performance Areas (MPAs): Executing the Mission, Leading People, Managing People, and Continuous Improvement

The final product of their work as reflected in 4 primary skill levels: Developing, Proficient, Highly Proficient, Exceptionally Skilled.

Demonstrated performance relative to their peers and informed by quantifiable accomplishments.
The correct answer is: Demonstrated performance in the 4 Major Performance Areas (MPAs): Executing the Mission, Leading People, Managing Resources, and Improving the Unit.

SAMPLE OF BEHAVIOR:  Describe what Active Listening is.

 

Select the best answer of when to use Active Listening:


The subordinate does not feel understood.

When a member is expressing frustration.

If a member is experiencing anger and is emotional about a problem.

A and C.

All of the above.

The correct answer is: All of the above. Active listening can be used when the subordinate does not feel understood, when a member is expressing frustration, and when a member is experiencing anger and is emotional about a problem.

SAMPLE OF BEHAVIOR:  Understand the differences between using I-CARE and LiSA CARE.

 

When using either technique, what is required by the “Ask” portion?



Determine if motivation or performance issues affect the issue.

Identify and provide resources to resolve the problem.

Collect and confirm facts

Confirm the person is aware of the problem.
The correct answer is: Collect and confirm facts. In both the I-CARE and LiSA CARE techniques, the "Ask" portion requires the individual to collect and confirm facts about the issue at hand.

SAMPLE OF BEHAVIOR:  Identify who is required to receive an EPR and OPB.

 

Which of the following is TRUE concerning Officer Performance Briefs?


Second Lieutenants will receive an Administrative LOE in lieu of an OPB when graduating from Officer Training School.

Captains may deny an OPB if they intend to request a Change of Reporting Official to avoid a negative rating.

Captains who have qualified for Major Select rank will receive their next evaluation according to the SCOD for Majors.

Non-USSF Captains’ OPBs will close out one year from the close-out date of the last evaluation.
The correct answer is: Non-USSF Captains’ OPBs will close out one year from the close-out date of the last evaluation.

SAMPLE OF BEHAVIOR:  Distinguish key principles of effective performance statements.

 

When it comes to writing performance statements for evaluations, supervisors should ensure they include all of the following components EXCEPT:


Action or behavior

Impact, outcome, or result

Evaluation of ALQ proficiency

Evaluation of AFC proficiency

The correct answer is: Evaluation of ALQ proficiency. Supervisors should include action or behavior, impact, outcome, or result in the performance statements. However, the evaluation of ALQ (Additional Duty Assignment) proficiency is not a necessary component.

SAMPLE OF BEHAVIOR:  Recognize that each counseling situation is unique for personal versus performance problems.

 

Which counseling approach is most effective for performance issues only?


I-CARE

LiSA CARE

Both A and B

None of the above
The correct answer is: I-CARE. The I-CARE counseling approach is most effective for performance issues only. LiSA CARE is specifically designed for personal problems.

SAMPLE OF BEHAVIOR:  Recall the promotion methods for Enlisted and Officer personnel.

 

To promote to Senior Airman, members must have:


12 months time-in-service

Obtained their 3-skill level

Obtained their 5-skill level

12 months time-in-grade

The correct answer is: 12 months time-in-service and obtained their 5-skill level. To promote to Senior Airman, members must have completed 12 months of time in service and obtained their 5-skill level.

SAMPLE OF BEHAVIOR:  Recognize that each counseling situation is unique for personal versus performance problems.

 

Is it ever practical to transition from one technique to another?

 


Yes, when a supervisor discovers that the root of an issue is personal rather than professional

Yes, when the supervisor is more skilled in one technique versus the other.

No, a supervisor should never transition from one technique to another.

Yes, if a subordinate does not respond to the initial technique used.

The correct answer is: Yes, if a subordinate does not respond to the initial technique used. It is practical to transition from one counseling technique to another if a subordinate does not respond to the initial technique used.

SAMPLE OF BEHAVIOR:  Recognize an Officer’s responsibilities to subordinates in the career progression system.

 

As an Officer, you have a responsibility to your subordinates to provide:


Career development counseling and discuss goals and how to achieve them.

Sufficient opportunity and time for self-development through education or formal training.

Timely counsel through Airman Comprehensive Assessments and verbal feedback.

A and B.

All the above.

The correct answer is: All the above. As an Officer, you have a responsibility to your subordinates to provide career development counseling, discuss goals and how to achieve them, provide sufficient opportunity and time for self-development through education or formal training, and offer timely counsel through Airman Comprehensive Assessments and verbal feedback.
